title: Several Labour MPs in talks with Greens about defecting to the party, sources say
description: Exclusive: none of the MPs are yet near point of crossing the floor and want guarantees they would be reselected for their seat at next election
content:
Several Labour MPs are in talks about defecting to the Greens, but are seeking guarantees they would be backed electorally by their new party, the Guardian has been told.
Zack Polanski, the leader of the Greens in England and Wales, has said publicly... [2928 chars]

title: How Iran war ship attacks choked off Strait of Hormuz
description: Six ships have been reportedly attacked in the Gulf in less than 48 hours, bringing the total vessels attacked during the war to 18.
content:
Six ships have been attacked in the Gulf in less than 48 hours, bringing the total of vessels attacked since the start of the war to 18, according to reports by UK Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) and the maritime intelligence company Vanguard.
It c... [4846 chars]

title: British ceramics firm Denby to appoint administrators
description: The famous company said rising costs had "squeezed the business financially".
content:
Renowned 217-year-old pottery firm Denby has confirmed plans to appoint administrators saying it has struggled with rising costs in recent years.
The Derbyshire-based company was founded in 1809 and bought by the investment firm Hilco Capital 2009, s... [1052 chars]

title: Ukrainians who ran 'premium' migrant smuggling service to UK jailed
description: Oleksandr Yavtushenko, 43, and Vladyslav Cherniavskyi, 38, were given five and six years respectively. They charged up to 15,000 euros a head.
content:
Two Ukrainian men who ran a "premium bespoke taxi service" smuggling migrants across the channel in a yacht have been jailed.
Vladyslav Cherniavskyi, 38, was jailed for six years and Oleksandr Yavtushenko, 43, for five having previously pleaded guilt... [2416 chars]

title: Iran vows to fight on in first message issued in name of Mojtaba Khamenei
description: Tehran will keep strait of Hormuz closed and continue attacks on US assets, speech read out on state TV says
content:
Iran issued its first message in the name of its new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, on Thursday, saying it would keep the strait of Hormuz closed and continue to attack US bases in the region.
The missive was read out on state TV rather than deliv... [3783 chars]
